title: Clean, Modern, and Declarative JavaScript

learningObjectives.text: Explain readable code using a new example and justify the result.
Compare declarative versus imperative style using a new example and justify the result.
Explain functional principles using a new example and justify the result.
Explain refactoring practices using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: The ideas in clean, modern, and declarative javascript recur throughout later JavaScript work, reducing memorization and debugging later. It belongs to the **Modern JavaScript Development** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Readable code** → **Declarative versus imperative style** → **Functional principles** → **Refactoring practices**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: Readable code
sections.body: Readable code communicates intent through naming, coherent units, consistent shapes, and limited hidden mutation.

sections.title: Declarative versus imperative style
sections.body: Imperative code emphasizes steps; declarative code emphasizes the desired transformation or state. Use the clearest level of abstraction.

sections.title: Functional principles
sections.body: Functional principles favor pure transformations, immutability, explicit inputs, and composition where they improve predictability.

sections.title: Refactoring practices
sections.body: Refactoring improves internal structure while preserving observable behavior. Work in small verified steps and stop when the design is clearer.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: The declarative pipeline states the selection and transformation directly.

commonMistakes: Memorizing syntax without predicting the underlying value or execution order.
Mutating shared data when the task expects a new value or explicit ownership.
Treating browser APIs and language features as the same layer.
Choosing an abstraction from habit rather than required operations.
summary: Clean, Modern, and Declarative JavaScript connects Readable code, Declarative versus imperative style, Functional principles.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

assessmentProfile.label: Mental model plus practical code reasoning
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic combines an important model with implementation consequences, so both explanation and code reasoning are required.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
